---
title: "02 Sampling profile"
subtitle: "Summarizing sampling characteristics of nautilus observations"
author: "Whitney Goodell"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 7
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: false
---

# Overview

This script gives an idea of the ocean profile of nautilus observations.  

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
#| label: setup

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readxl)
library(PristineSeasR2)

ps_paths <- ps_science_paths()
exp_path <- file.path(ps_paths$projects, "prj-nautilus-paper")

# where data comes from and where it goes
raw_dir       <- file.path(exp_path, "data/raw")
processed_dir <- file.path(exp_path, "data/processed")

# Load the cleaned data
df_clean <- read_csv(file.path(processed_dir, "nautilus_data_cleaned.csv"))
```

# Vertical sampling profile  
```{r}
#| label: vertical-sampling-profile

# 0. Prep the data: Force X-axis order and Title Case for display
df_clean <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(
    method = case_when(
      method == "dropcam" ~ "Dropcam",
      method == "sub"     ~ "Sub",
      method == "rov"     ~ "ROV",
      TRUE ~ method
    ),
    method = factor(method, levels = c("Dropcam", "Sub", "ROV")),
    baited = str_to_title(baited) # Capitalizes 'baited' and 'unbaited'
  )

# 1. Calculate n-counts for labels above boxplots
# This step is cleanest way to get "n = X" labels in the plot without cluttering the main data.
n_labels <- df_clean %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    # label at top of plot
    y_pos = 0 
  )


# 2. Create depth profile plot
ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = method, y = depth)) +
  # boxplot color-coded by baiting status
  # outlier.shape = NA - will use geom_jitter to show all points, so NA will prevent double-counting
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = baited), width = 0.6, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  
  # Individual observations
  geom_jitter(color = "darkblue", alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1, size = 3) +
  
  # Labels: n = X above boxes
  geom_text(data = n_labels, aes(y = y_pos, label = paste0("n = ", count)), 
            vjust = 2, fontface = "bold", size = 5, color = "navyblue") +
  
  # depth profile plot, with shallow (0 m) at top
  scale_y_reverse(limits = c(600, 0), breaks = seq(0, 600, 100)) +
  
  # Manual colors for Baited vs Unbaited
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baited" = "gold", "Unbaited" = "lightgreen")) +
  
  # Labels and title
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Depth (m)",
    fill = "Baited"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # theme and font refinements
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 20)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", margin = margin(r = 20)),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black", face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
    
    # Legend formatting
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "right",
    
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16)
  )
```


# Species distribution table  
```{r}
#| label: spp-distrib-table 

df_clean %>%
  group_by(country, species) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  knitr::kable(caption = "Species occurrence counts across Pacific EEZs")
```
